title: Can we dissociate hypervigilance to social threats from altered perceptual decision-making processes in lonely individuals? An exploration with Drift Diffusion Modelling and event-related potentials.
description: This dataset investigates the dissociation between hypervigilance to social threats and altered perceptual decision-making in lonely individuals using event-related potentials (ERP) and Drift Diffusion Modeling. The study combines electrophysiological recordings with computational modeling to examine whether loneliness-related social threat sensitivity reflects genuine perceptual changes or decision-level alterations in threat processing.
methods_description: Data collection involved event-related potential (ERP) recordings during a perceptual decision-making task with social threat stimuli. Drift Diffusion Modeling was applied to behavioral responses to dissociate perceptual sensitivity from decision criteria.

affiliations.name: Social Neuroscience Lab, Institute of Psychology Polish Academy of Sciences  Warsaw Poland
